overview: Selesnya Gearhulk is a ramp-focused strategy that leverages land acceleration and creature scaling to outvalue opponents. By playing efficient early-game creatures and using land-searching tools, the deck transitions into high-impact threats like Ouroboroid. The goal is to establish an unblockable board presence and win through combat damage.
win_conditions: Combat damage from large creatures like Ouroboroid
Overwhelming the board with scaled creatures and tokens
Ramping into multiple threats per turn to bypass interaction

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and a one-drop or two-drop
Hands containing Keen-Eyed Curator or early creatures
Hands that can play a threat by turn three
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritize fetchable duals or basics that enter untapped), plus at least one of Badgermole Cub, Ouroboroid, Keen-Eyed Curator, or Gearhulk.
Keep hands with 3+ mana sources and a clear path to casting a 3-4 drop on turn 2 or 3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands
Hands with only high-cost cards like Ouroboroid
Hands lacking early interaction or ramp
Mulligan pure curve decks, hands with only 1-2 lands, or hands lacking early artifacts/creatures to stabilize.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and play Keen-Eyed Curator to accelerate mana
Cast small creatures like Badgermole Cub to establish board presence
Turn 1: Play a land, ideally a fetch or basic that sets up W/G mana.
Turn 2: Cast a 2-drop artifact or mana rock (e.g., Arcane Signet, Sol Ring, or early Cub/Ouroboroid if mana allows).
Turn 3: Aim to cast Gearhulk or Keen-Eyed Curator to dig for more artifacts.
Sequence lands to ensure double-colored mana for Gearhulk by turn 3-4, prioritizing untapped sources to maintain tempo.
mid_game: Use Curator to fetch additional lands and smooth out mana base
Begin casting larger threats and leverage creature synergies
Block efficiently while building toward a decisive turn
Prioritize keeping artifacts in the graveyard for Gearhulk recursion.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if you lack protection or answers.
Manage mana to ensure 4WWG is available for Gearhulk without flooding.
Use Keen-Eyed Curator to dig for specific artifacts or Gearhulk itself, and track graveyard contents to maximize recursion value.
Commit threats when you have removal or counters to protect them, or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back Gearhulk if the opponent has sweepers or heavy removal.
late_game: Cast Ouroboroid and other high-cost finishers
Deploy multiple creatures per turn to overwhelm blockers
Close out the game with combat damage before opponent can stabilize
Gearhulk returning a large artifact/creature, Keen-Eyed Curator finding a win-con, or a late-game artifact like Thran Dynamo combined with a big creature.
The goal is to stabilize and then swing with protected threats or combo off with artifact synergy.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent is low on removal, or you can protect your board with counters/removal.
Commit when you can safely cast Gearhulk and get a free value engine, or when you can force the opponent to use multiple answers on a single threat.

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.role: Early-game creature that scales or provides board presence
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to apply early pressure and grow into a relevant threat; use as a blocker or attacker depending on matchup

key_cards.name: Ouroboroid
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have sufficient mana; leverage its death trigger to generate tokens and maintain board control

key_cards.name: Keen-Eyed Curator
key_cards.notes: Use its ability to fetch basic lands or put lands onto the battlefield to accelerate into big spells; keep it on board to enable turn-three threats

sequencing_tips: Hold up mana for potential interaction before committing to large spells
Play Curator before casting expensive creatures to ensure mana availability
Time Ouroboroid's entrance to maximize its death trigger value
Prioritize land drops over unnecessary creature trades in the early game
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the board without protection.
Gearhulk is highly vulnerable to sweepers, so always keep mana open for protection or recursion.
Badgermole Cub and Ouroboroid are critical for fueling the engine and generating mana/boards.
Keen-Eyed Curator is a powerful dig spell that can find Gearhulk or key artifacts.
Don't forget to fetch lands for Gearhulk's mana cost, and track the graveyard carefully to maximize recursion value.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early blockers and use Curator to ramp past their curve; avoid trading large creatures early.
Ramp quickly to bypass counterspells and deploy threats that are hard to remove; use Curator to find lands consistently.
Race them by playing efficiently and leveraging creature scaling; use Ouroboroid to outvalue their removal.

Use cheap artifacts to pressure, cast Gearhulk at end step or with mana open for protection. Cast multiple threats to force through, and use artifact spells that are hard to counter or cast in response to their counterspells.

Apply early pressure with artifacts, use removal to stop key ramp pieces, and stabilize with board wipes or counters. Don't let them get too far ahead in mana, and prioritize disrupting their mana acceleration over trading creatures.

Win by having better resource management, more answers, and better graveyard setup. Sideboard in more artifact hate and counters. Win the attrition war by protecting your Gearhulks, out-drawing them, and forcing unfavorable trades where you have more recursion options.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), artifact hate (e.g., Disenchant, Abrade), and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents are holding up mana for counters or removal.
Track graveyard contents to know what Gearhulk can return, and monitor opponent's hand size to gauge their ability to interact with your key threats.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Selesnya Gearhulk, W/G, Badgermole Cub, Ouroboroid, Keen-Eyed Curator
